items.title: Compact Latent Coordination for Autonomous Vehicles at Unsignalized Intersections
items.summary: This hierarchical reinforcement learning framework simplifies multi-agent coordination for autonomous vehicles by using a centralized master agent to generate compact global strategies for decentralized workers.
items.authors: Gil Lifshits, Igal Bilik, Gilad Katz

items.title: Agentic coding without the cloud: evaluating open-weight large language models on longitudinal data preparation tasks
items.summary: This framework evaluates open-weight LLMs for local data processing tasks, providing a secure way to automate research workflows without exposing sensitive data to third-party cloud services.
items.authors: Mack Nixon, Liam Wright, Yevgeniya Kovalchuk et al.

items.title: Euclid-MCP: A Model Context Protocol Server for Deterministic Logical Reasoning via Prolog
items.summary: Euclid-MCP bridges the gap between LLM reasoning and symbolic logic by providing a standardized interface to link neural models with Prolog for reliable, deterministic decision-making.
items.authors: Bartolomeo Bogliolo

items.title: MedGame: Storytelling Gamification Empowered by Large Language Models for Medical Education
items.summary: MedGame uses LLMs to turn static clinical case studies into interactive, decision-based storytelling games, offering a more engaging and structured approach to medical training.
items.authors: Qian Wu, Xinrong Zhou, Zizhan Ma et al.

items.title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference
items.summary: PyroDash reduces inference costs by using a small model to generate easy tokens and selectively offloading complex reasoning to a large model, optimizing performance without retraining.
items.authors: Niqi Lyu, Pengtao Shi, Wei Qiu et al.

items.title: LKValues: Aligning Large Language Models with Sri Lankan Societal Values
items.summary: This research provides a dataset and framework to align language models with Sri Lankan cultural values, addressing the Western bias common in current AI alignment benchmarks.
items.authors: Nethmi Muthugala, Supryadi, Surangika Ranathunga et al.

items.title: Persian Pixel: A large-scale synthetic OCR dataset for Persian language
items.summary: Persian Pixel provides a large-scale synthetic dataset to help improve OCR accuracy for the complex, cursive nature of the Persian writing system.
items.authors: Pouria Mahdi, Haq Nawaz Malik

items.title: Notes to Self: Can LLMs Benefit from Experiential Abstractions?
items.summary: The authors demonstrate that LLMs perform better when they store and retrieve high-level strategies from past experiences, mimicking how humans learn to solve problems more efficiently.
items.authors: Chang Liu, Xinyu Li, Artur Dubrawski

items.title: Toward Auditable Fraud Detection: Combining Graph Features, Model Explanations, and Agentic Case Investigation
items.summary: This paper proposes a fraud detection framework that uses graph features and LLM-driven agents to explain and investigate suspicious transactions, helping teams build more transparent and accountable systems.
items.authors: Rahil Sharma

items.title: In-Context Time Series Classification with Random Convolutional Features
items.summary: The authors show that combining random convolutional features with pretrained tabular foundation models improves time series classification, offering a robust alternative to traditional linear classifiers.
items.authors: Joscha Cüppers, Jilles Vreeken

items.title: Assessment in Team Problem-Solving Exercises in Computing Education
items.summary: This study explores using recorded team interaction data to automate and improve the assessment of collaborative problem-solving exercises, providing a path for more efficient performance evaluation.
items.authors: Valdemar Švábenský, Jan Vykopal, Sukrit Leelaluk et al.

items.title: Copy Less, Ground More: Overcoming Repetitive Copying in Long-Context Reasoning via Evidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: The researchers introduce a reinforcement learning method to stop LLMs from mindlessly copying long-context inputs, forcing models to focus on relevant evidence for better reasoning performance.
items.authors: Lizhe Fang, Weizhou Shen, Tianyi Tang et al.

items.title: SGA: Plug&Play Geometric Verification for Educational Video Synthesis
items.summary: This tool automatically detects and fixes spatial errors in LLM-generated educational animations, ensuring that visual elements do not overlap incorrectly.
items.authors: Lopez Jhon, Hinojosa Carlos, Ghanem Bernard

items.title: The Shared Discovery Paradox: How a One-Answer Rule Turns Better Information into Worse Search
items.summary: This study reveals that sharing a single best-guess recommendation among a group can paradoxically reduce overall search success compared to decentralized, diverse exploration.
items.authors: Yohei Nakajima

items.title: Natural Language Access to Domain-Specific Metadata: A Reusable Framework for LLM Query Generation
items.summary: By mapping domain-specific metadata to an ontology, you can enable LLMs to generate accurate database queries without needing expensive fine-tuning or complex retrieval setups.
items.authors: Blake G. Fitch, Cato Elia Kurtz

items.title: Vector Search As Nearest Neighbor Matching: RAG-based Policy Learning in Causal Inference
items.summary: This framework treats RAG as a nearest-neighbor matching problem, allowing data scientists to optimize decision-making policies by retrieving and analyzing action-specific evidence.
items.authors: Masahiro Kato, Taka Kato

items.title: CRAFT: Clustering Rubrics to Diagnose Weak LLM Capabilities and Generate Targeted Fine-Tuning Data
items.summary: The authors introduce a method to diagnose specific LLM weaknesses by analyzing rubric-based evaluations, helping practitioners generate targeted training data to fix model failures.
items.authors: Vipul Gupta, Zihao Wang, Razvan-Gabriel Dumitru et al.

items.title: LLM-Powered Agentic AI for 5G/6G Networks: A Tutorial and Survey on Architectures, Protocols, and Standardization
items.summary: This survey explores how agentic AI can automate 5G and 6G network management, providing a roadmap for integrating autonomous reasoning and planning into telecommunications infrastructure.
items.authors: Mazene Ameur, Abdelkader Mekrache, Bouziane Brik et al.

items.title: BayesPO: Bayesian Prompt Optimization via Parallel-Tempered Gradient-Guided Discrete MCMC
items.summary: The researchers treat prompt optimization as a Bayesian sampling problem, offering a more rigorous mathematical approach to finding effective instructions without retraining model weights.
items.authors: Junjie Zhou, Zhijian Ou

items.title: Knowledge-Guided Cross-Modal Fusion for Adult-to-Pediatric ECG Transfer via Label-Conditioned Contrastive Alignment
items.summary: This framework improves pediatric ECG diagnosis by using knowledge-guided alignment to bridge the data gap between adult-heavy training sets and the unique requirements of pediatric patients.
items.authors: Xinran Liu, Yuwen Li, Hongxiang Gao et al.

items.title: Digital Pantheon: Simulating and Auditing Coalition Formation with LLM Agents
items.summary: This framework uses fine-tuning and retrieval to help LLM agents maintain consistent ideological stances, offering a way to simulate complex political negotiations without the usual neutrality bias.
items.authors: Dylan Van Mulders, Matthias Bogaert, Dirk Van den Poel

items.title: SearchOS-V1: Towards Robust Open-Domain Information-Seeking Agent Collaboration
items.summary: SearchOS improves information-seeking agents by using a persistent, shared state to prevent repetitive search loops, making automated web research more reliable and efficient.
items.authors: Yuyao Zhang, Junjie Gao, Zhengxian Wu et al.

items.title: Mutable Low-Rank Sketches for Retrain-Free Recommendation
items.summary: This method uses mutable low-rank sketches to update user embeddings in real-time without full model retraining, significantly reducing staleness in recommendation systems.
items.authors: Hector J. Garcia, Nick Clayton

items.title: Expanding the Lexicon of Ge'ez Based African Languages: A Comparative Study of Amharic and Tigrinya
items.summary: By creating language-specific tokenizers for Ge'ez-script languages, this research improves the performance of multilingual models on low-resource African languages suffering from poor subword fragmentation.
items.authors: Hailay Kidu Teklehaymanot, Debela Desalegn Yadeta, Wolfgang Nejdl

items.title: Experience Memory Graph: One-Shot Error Correction for Agents
items.summary: This method uses a graph-based memory structure to help AI agents fix errors in one shot, reducing the high costs and latency of iterative self-correction loops.
items.authors: Wenjun Wang, Yuchen Fang, Fengrui Liu et al.

items.title: Unleashing Multimodal Large Language Models for Training-free HOI Detection in the Wild
items.summary: The authors introduce a training-free approach for detecting human-object interactions that allows models to generalize to new scenarios without needing task-specific labeled data.
items.authors: Ting Lei, Jialin Liu, Zhu Xu et al.

items.title: Screening of Biosecurity Features in Metagenomic Data with Evo 2 Probes
items.summary: Researchers demonstrate that simple linear probes on genomic foundation models can effectively identify biosecurity threats like antimicrobial resistance without requiring expensive model fine-tuning.
items.authors: Jeremy Guntoro, Alexander Dack, Dylan Danno et al.

items.title: AI-accelerated End-to-End Framework for Rapid Professional Upskilling
items.summary: This framework integrates AI across the entire professional training lifecycle to significantly speed up content creation and skill assessment for enterprise upskilling.
items.authors: Tam Nguyen, Hung Nguyen, Robert Ogburn
